step2.min3chars: Must be at least 3 characters long.
step2.max20chars: Must be at most 20 characters long.
step2.onlyLettersAndNumbers: Only letters and numbers are allowed.

step3.hint: You are estimated to be alerted around <alertAmount /> with these settings.

step4.title: Download Pushsafer
step4.subtitle: For AlertAigües to work, you need to download Pushsafer and grant permission for both normal and critical notifications. Location services are not required and can remain disabled. When you are finished, return to this page. If you have already done this, you can skip this step.

step6.subtitle: Follow the instructions to add your device to Pushsafer and complete the setup.
step6.warningHint: Make sure you have the Pushsafer app installed on this device.
step6.qrCodeTitle: Open the Pushsafer app <arrow></arrow> Scroll down to 'Register Guest Device' <arrow></arrow> Click on 'Scan QR Code' <arrow></arrow> Scan this code:
step6.buttonTitle: If you have installed Pushsafer, click this button to automatically open Pushsafer and add your configured account. You will then receive a notification to verify that your alert system is working:

landing.subtitle: Receive instant critical notifications for water flow and level changes, with customisable alert values to suit your needs.

help.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about AlertAigua.

iosAndroid.question: Is there a difference between the iOS and Android versions?
iosAndroid.answer: Yes. On iOS, if you enable critical notifications in the Pushsafer app, you will receive alerts even when your phone is on silent or Do Not Disturb mode is active. On Android, this behaviour must be configured manually in your system notification settings.

dataSource.question: Where does the data come from?
dataSource.answer: We use official data from SAIH Ebro — the Sistema Automático de Información Hidrológica, operated by the Confederación Hidrográfica del Ebro, the Spanish government authority responsible for managing the Ebro river basin.

safety.question: Is this system 100% safe?
safety.answer: No — and no system is. We receive data directly from SAIH Ebro. Occasionally, measuring stations go offline or experience technical difficulties. We do not take any responsibility for damages of any kind. The source code is open-source, so if you discover a potential issue in the code, please open an issue on GitHub. We tested the system for a full year and encountered zero bugs. The SAIH Ebro station was online approximately 97.4% of the time during that period.

whyBuilt.question: Why was AlertAigua built?
whyBuilt.answer: This notification service was built because several people from Arens de Lledó had to manually check water levels and flow rates every single day to protect themselves, their animals, their land, and their tools from sudden floods — events that happen more frequently now than in the past.

support.title: Still have questions?
support.description: Couldn't find what you were looking for? Browse the source code or reach out directly.

step1.title: Download Pushsafer
step1.description: AlertAigua delivers its notifications through Pushsafer, a trusted push-notification service. Grab it free from your app store and open it once to create your device key.

step2.title: Configure your alarm settings
step2.description: In the AlertAigua setup, choose which water level and flow values should trigger an alert. Set your own thresholds so you only hear about changes that actually matter to you.

step3.title: Add AlertAigua to Pushsafer
step3.description: Link the two with a single tap — press the “Add to Pushsafer” button, or scan the QR code from your phone. That’s the whole connection; no copying keys by hand.

step4.title: Receive alerts when flooding threatens
step4.description: After your setup is complete, you’re set. The moment river levels cross your thresholds, AlertAigua pushes a critical notification straight to your phone — day or night — so you can act before the water reaches you.
